message: Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it. Heat, cornmeal crust, acid cutting through the fat - there's actual technique here if we don't turn it into a circus act.

The heat-and-acid thing Margaret just said is actually brilliant and Steph feels a flutter of relief that someone else sees the potential - but she's also second-guessing whether she should have pushed this idea herself last week when she had it in her Google Doc, and now she's worried that agreeing too quickly makes her look like she's just rubber-stamping whatever Margaret says, which is exactly the opposite of what she's trying to prove, so she needs to acknowledge Margaret's insight while also showing she's thought this through independently, but not so much that it sounds like she's defending herself, which would be weird, so maybe she just... agrees and adds something that shows she gets it? I was actually thinking about the technical angle - the cornmeal texture against a really crispy exterior could be the whole story if we nail it.

Yeah, the texture story works - but what if we lean into the fact that corn dogs are fundamentally about hiding something good inside something fried, and the jalapeno is the surprise that refuses to hide?
That's smart - the surprise angle gives us something to actually talk about instead of just "spicy corn dog."
The heat hitting after that first crunch - that's the moment. We could write the whole thing around the anticipation before the bite, not just the payoff.
So we're locked on the anticipation-first angle, heat as the reveal - I think that's our lane this week. Good call getting us there.
